title: hyptis rugosa var. canescens
intro: Hyptis rugosa var. canescens, also known as Brazilian rosemary or white hyptis, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating wounds, due to its high concentration of essential oils, particularly carvacrol and thymol. Hyptis rugosa var. canescens can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, essential oils, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in different ways.
